evidence.note: R1 should be exactly 28 bp (16 bp cell barcode + 12 bp UMI)

findings.title: Cell barcodes on the 10x whitelist

evidence.note: R1[0:16] should match the 3M-february-2018 gel-bead barcode whitelist

findings.detail: 94.9% of cell barcodes are on the whitelist

findings.title: R2 reads beginning with the TSO (adapter-dimer / short insert)

evidence.note: R2 should start with cDNA; a leading TSO is the hallmark of empty/short-insert products

findings.detail: 3.8% of R2 start with the TSO

findings.title: R2 read-through into the Illumina adapter

evidence.note: the AGATCGGAAGAGC adapter stem in R2 means the insert was shorter than the read length

findings.detail: 0.1% of R2 contain the adapter stem AGATCGGAAGAGC

findings.title: R2 with a poly-G no-signal tail

evidence.note: a poly-G 3' tail on two-color instruments is 'no signal' — typical of empty/short fragments

findings.detail: 0.2% of R2 have a poly-G tail

ranked.why: 3.84% of R2 reads begin with the TSO, the highest-scoring finding (severity 0.0769) and the only one within striking distance of its threshold (< 0.05). Per read_structure.R2.readthrough_chain[tso_5prime], a leading TSO marks empty or short-insert products, so this fraction is the library's main (still sub-threshold) source of non-cDNA reads.

0.16% of R2 carry a poly-G no-signal tail against a < 0.01 threshold — roughly 6x of headroom. Consistent with the small empty/short-fragment population implied by the TSO rate, not with a two-color no-signal problem.
Only 0.08% of R2 contain the AGATCGGAAGAGC adapter stem against a < 0.02 threshold. Inserts are essentially all longer than the 91 bp read, so size selection worked.

94.89% of R1[0:16] barcodes match the 3M-february-2018 gel-bead whitelist, comfortably above the >= 0.85 threshold. Barcode chemistry and read offsets are correctly assigned; the ~5% off-whitelist remainder is the expected sequencing-error background.
R1 is a uniform 28 bp (min = modal = max), exactly the 16 bp cell barcode + 12 bp UMI required by read_structure.R1 for v3/v3.1. No trimming, no run-configuration mismatch.

plan.root_cause: No failure detected — all five checks pass, and the only finding with meaningful signal (3.84% TSO at R2 start, threshold < 0.05) is best explained by the normal low-level background of empty-GEM / short-insert products inherent to 10x v3 chemistry, not by a prep or sequencing fault.
plan.diagnosis: This library looks healthy: R1 is exactly 28 bp as v3/v3.1 requires, 94.9% of cell barcodes hit the 3M-february-2018 whitelist, and adapter read-through (0.08%) and poly-G tails (0.16%) are both an order of magnitude under their thresholds — so barcode chemistry, insert size, and the sequencing run are all behaving. The one thing worth watching is that 3.8% of R2 reads start with the TSO, which is under the 5% cutoff but is the largest non-cDNA fraction present; these are empty or very short inserts that will be discarded at alignment. No bench action is needed on this library, but if the TSO fraction climbs past 5% on the next prep from the same GEM run, tighten the post-cDNA SPRI size selection to drop the short products before indexing.
